kleiner-perkins.overview: Kleiner Perkins is a venture firm, among the most active investors we track. Its stated focus: Seed → growth; enterprise, consumer, AI, hard tech.
kleiner-perkins.six_months: Over the last six months we tracked 45 investments and 7 posts/theses from Kleiner Perkins, investing at a rapid clip.
kleiner-perkins.thesis: The money is concentrated in Enterprise software (20 deals), followed by AI agents & apps (6 deals), Robotics & industrial (4 deals). That points to a thesis centered on broad enterprise software and applied AI. There's a genuine second leg in AI agents & apps (autonomous agents and applied-AI products that do real work), so this isn't a one-note fund.
kleiner-perkins.how: By stage, recent checks skew Series A — backing companies right as they scale.
kleiner-perkins.notable: Standout recent bets include Saronic ($1.8B), Mind Robotics ($400M), Rivian spinoff Mind Robotics ($400M), Stord ($250M), Amazon fulfillment competitor Stord ($250M). Their largest tracked check went to Saronic ($1.8B), a read on where their conviction sits right now.
kleiner-perkins.watch: For Smith Point, the overlap is clearest in Enterprise software, AI agents & apps, Robotics & industrial and they're a fund we can often reach founders through. Worth watching: continued concentration in Enterprise software suggests more of the same ahead, while rising AI agents & apps activity is the emerging tell.
